entry.title: PPDS location created as suffix_01 & same location maintained as Business partner in TM
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

We are currently having a scenario where in the embedded TM system is already implemented and live in production environment. As a part of global implementation, we are now implementing the PPDS in S4. While integrating the production plants we encountered with below scenario, and we need your valuable inputs on how to proceed ahead.

Scenario:

  1. Plant which we want to use in PPDS is already created as business partner in embedded TM environment in S4.
  2. The solution for TM is live in Production
  3. We integrated the 1000 with location type 1001 into PPDS.
  4. It comes as 1000_01, this is observed via standard integration result in PPDS as 1000 (BP location type = 1021) is already used by TM as one time location

To address below points :

  1. Have we encountered this scenario before ?
  2. Does this work as standard behaviour for MD and TD on PPDS side ?
  3. Any challenges/implications/limitations we are aware of if we have done this in past?

Thanks

Debasis Nanda

entry.title: Re: SAC Broadcast Report Zip File
entry.content:
Hi @GeorgeBennett ,

No, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) automatically zips emailed burst reports for security and compression reasons, and there is no standard option to send them as unzipped attachments.

However, here are some potential workarounds:

1.Manually Extract and Resend: The recipient can manually extract and forward the report as an unzipped attachment.
2.Use a File Repository: Store the report in a shared location (e.g., SAP Business Technology Platform, SharePoint, or Google Drive) and email a direct link instead of an attachment.

default email sender configured in the SAC tenant settings.

Kindly upvote.

Thanks

entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to retrieve master data using master data retrieval API for procurement and the results were not returning correctly. 

1. Using the Search endpoint (/entities/{entityName})- there are entities that are returning results for example, entity User will show the results but for CostCenter, Business Unit it just return one value. See screenshot. The count shows there are 537 but only one was returned.

    "errMessage": "The following fields are not valid in $select: [Name]"
 

MetaData Result:

entry.title: Re: No data was released for version Y10 and Consolidation Unit - SAP Group Reporting
entry.content:
System shows this warning if there is no more data to release from ACDOCA to GR. Here, I see that you are doing a FULL release, yet you are not getting any data into Group Reporting.
1) Please check if you have any data for this period and Consolidation Unit in ACDOCA.
2) If you don't have data in ACDOCA, then the warning message is justified.
3) But, if you have data in ACDOCA, but still not getting any data into GR after Data Release, that means the Consolidation Related Fields (Cons Unit, FS Item, SubItem, Partner Unit etc.,) are not derived for these lines in ACDOCA. Please verify. If this is the case, then you first run Realignment job to have these Consolidation Related fields populated in ACDOCA. Then do a Data Release to Group Reporting.

entry.title: 💰 SAP Salaries in 2025: Europe vs. USA - Are You Earning Your Worth? 💰
entry.content:
Are you an SAP professional wondering how your compensation stacks up in today's dynamic market? In 2025, the demand for skilled SAP consultants continues to surge, making it a fantastic time to be in the field. However, the salary landscape varies significantly depending on your location. Let's dive into a comparative analysis of SAP salaries in Europe and the USA to see how they stack up!

Key Insights from our 2025 SAP Salary Snapshot:

  • 🌍 USA vs. Europe: Generally, SAP professionals in the USA tend to command higher salaries compared to their European counterparts. This remains a consistent trend in 2025.
  • 📈 Experience Matters: Experience is a major salary driver on both sides of the Atlantic. As you climb the career ladder to Senior and Lead roles, you can expect substantial increases in your earning potential in both Europe and the USA.
  • 🔥 High Demand Continues: The SAP ecosystem is thriving! The demand for experienced SAP consultants is robust across all modules and technologies in both Europe and the USA.

Factors Shaping SAP Salaries in 2025:

  • 🇪🇺 Europe: European SAP salaries are influenced by a mix of factors:
    • Cost of Living Variations: Salary levels reflect the diverse cost of living across different European countries.
    • Social Benefits & Packages: Robust social security and comprehensive benefits packages in many European nations can influence base salary expectations.
    • Multilingual Skills: In multilingual Europe, language skills can be a valuable asset and impact compensation.
  • 🇺🇸 USA: The US SAP market is characterized by:
    • High Market Demand: A strong and competitive market for top SAP talent drives salaries upwards.
    • Location-Based Variations: Major tech hubs in the USA often offer premium compensation packages to attract skilled professionals.

Are you getting paid what you deserve in the 2025 SAP market?

  • SAP Consultants: Take a moment to compare your current compensation against the salary ranges presented in our snapshot. Are you positioned competitively?
  • Hiring Managers: Ensure your salary offers are attractive and competitive to secure and retain top-tier SAP talent in this demanding market.
  • Aspiring SAP Professionals: Understanding the earning potential and career growth trajectory within the SAP ecosystem is crucial for planning your career path.

Let's Discuss! What are your thoughts on these SAP salary trends for 2025? Do these insights align with your experiences? Share your perspectives and questions in the comments below! 👇

#SAP #SAPSalaries #SAPCareers #Europe #USA #Compensation #TechSalaries #SAPConsultant #S4HANA #SAPFICO #SAPMM #SAPSD #CareerDevelopment #LinkedInLearning #2025Salaries

Important Note: Remember, these salary figures are estimates and can fluctuate based on various factors including your specific location, specialized skills, industry sector, and the size of the company. The averages provided are based on common SAP module areas like FI, MM, and SD, and may not fully reflect niche or highly specialized roles which can command even higher compensation.

📊 Key Takeaways: 📊

➡️ USA SAP salaries are generally higher than in Europe.

➡️ Experience is a significant factor impacting earning potential in both regions.

➡️ The demand for skilled SAP professionals remains strong across the SAP ecosystem.

🌍 Regional Factors Influencing Salaries: 🌍

🇪🇺 Europe: Cost of living variations, social benefits, and valuable language skills.

🇺🇸 USA: Higher market demand, strong competition, and location in major tech hubs.

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_sap-sapsalaries-sapcareers-activity-7296155373273575424-x4It?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAAJPDL8BjpJe3thZB6t04k7Z2hRVHhboAec

entry.title: 🚀 Salaires des Consultants SAP en France en 2025 : Êtes-vous rémunéré à votre juste valeur ? 🚀
entry.content:
🚀 Salaires des Consultants SAP en France en 2025 : Êtes-vous rémunéré à votre juste valeur ? 🚀

Êtes-vous un consultant SAP talentueux naviguant dans le paysage professionnel français ? Vous vous demandez si votre salaire reflète véritablement votre expertise et la demande du marché ? En ce début d'année 2025, une chose est claire : le marché SAP en France reste extrêmement dynamique, et vos compétences sont plus précieuses que jamais ! Plongeons ensemble au cœur des tendances salariales pour les consultants SAP en France, en explorant les fourchettes de rémunération, les facteurs clés et les perspectives d'évolution, que vous soyez un profil junior ambitieux ou un expert multi-modules chevronné.

Ce qu'il faut retenir de notre analyse des salaires SAP en 2025 :

  • 📈 L'expérience, ça compte ! C'est une constante dans le monde SAP, et cela se confirme en 2025 : l'expérience est le principal moteur de votre salaire. Notre analyse révèle une progression salariale significative à chaque étape de votre carrière, du junior fraîchement diplômé à l'expert fort de nombreuses années de projets complexes. Cette valorisation de l'expérience souligne l'importance cruciale de l'apprentissage continu et du développement de vos compétences dans un écosystème SAP en constante évolution.

  • 🔥 Forte demande : Un marché français toujours aussi dynamique. Bonne nouvelle pour les professionnels SAP : le marché français du conseil SAP se maintient à un niveau élevé d'activité. La transformation digitale des entreprises françaises, accélérée par les enjeux de compétitivité et d'innovation, alimente une demande soutenue pour des profils SAP qualifiés. Plus particulièrement, l'expertise sur SAP S/4HANA, la suite ERP de nouvelle génération de SAP, est particulièrement recherchée et valorisée par les employeurs. Si vous êtes un expert S/4HANA, votre profil est assurément "hot" sur le marché !

  • 🎯 Spécialisation = valorisation : Devenez un expert recherché. Dans un marché SAP mature, la spécialisation est devenue un atout majeur pour optimiser votre rémunération. Les consultants SAP experts dans des modules spécifiques (Finance et Contrôle de Gestion - FI/CO, Gestion des Matériaux - MM, Ventes et Distribution - SD, Planification de la Production - PP, et bien d'autres) sont particulièrement courtisés par les entreprises. De même, les profils possédant une expertise multi-modules, capables de naviguer avec aisance entre différentes composantes de l'écosystème SAP, peuvent prétendre aux rémunérations les plus attractives. Investir dans le développement d'une expertise pointue est donc une stratégie payante pour booster votre carrière et votre salaire.

Quels facteurs influencent concrètement les salaires SAP en France ?

  • 📍 Localisation géographique : Paris et les métropoles en tête. Comme dans de nombreux secteurs, la localisation géographique joue un rôle non négligeable sur les salaires SAP en France. La région parisienne, cœur économique du pays, et les grandes métropoles régionales (Lyon, Lille, Marseille, Bordeaux, Nantes, etc.) offrent généralement des salaires plus élevés. Cela s'explique par un coût de la vie plus important dans ces zones urbaines, mais aussi par une plus forte concentration d'entreprises utilisatrices de SAP et de cabinets de conseil. Si vous êtes mobile géographiquement, n'hésitez pas à explorer les opportunités dans ces régions dynamiques.

  • 💼 Type d'employeur : ESN, Cabinets de Conseil ou Client Final ? Le type d'employeur pour lequel vous travaillez peut également impacter votre niveau de rémunération. On observe généralement des grilles salariales différentes entre les ESN (Entreprises de Services Numériques), les cabinets de conseil en management et en SI, et les clients finaux (entreprises utilisatrices de SAP). Chaque type de structure offre des avantages et des inconvénients en termes de salaire, de missions, et de perspectives de carrière. Il est important de bien comprendre ces différences pour faire le choix qui correspond le mieux à vos aspirations.

  • 🗣️ Compétences linguistiques : L'anglais, et au-delà ! Dans un contexte économique globalisé, la maîtrise de l'anglais est devenue un prérequis pour la plupart des consultants SAP en France. Cependant, dans un marché de plus en plus international, la connaissance d'autres langues étrangères (allemand, espagnol, etc.) peut devenir un véritable atout et impacter positivement votre rémunération. La capacité à évoluer dans des contextes multiculturels et à communiquer avec des équipes internationales est de plus en plus valorisée.

Consultant SAP en France : Êtes-vous payé à votre juste valeur en 2025 ?

Il est temps de faire le point sur votre situation personnelle ! Comparez votre salaire actuel aux fourchettes de rémunération présentées dans notre analyse. N'hésitez pas à échanger avec votre manager sur votre évolution salariale et les perspectives de progression.

  • Managers : Pour attirer et fidéliser les meilleurs talents SAP, assurez-vous de proposer des rémunérations compétitives et des packages attractifs (avantages sociaux, formation, mobilité...). Un consultant SAP épanoui est un atout précieux pour la performance de votre entreprise !

  • Jeunes diplômés et consultants en devenir : L'écosystème SAP en France offre de réelles opportunités de carrière et de progression salariale. Visualisez votre potentiel d'évolution et investissez dans le développement de vos compétences pour atteindre vos objectifs !

N'hésitez pas à partager votre expérience et vos insights sur les salaires SAP en France en commentaires ! Échangeons ensemble sur les tendances du marché et les meilleures stratégies pour optimiser votre rémunération ! 👇

#SAP #SalairesSAP #ConsultantSAP #France #MarchéDuTravail #Rémunération #S4HANA #SAPFICO #SAPMM #SAPSD #CarrièreIT #ConseilSAP #LinkedInLearning #Salaires2025 #FrenchTech #EcosystèmeSAP

📊 Points Clés à Retenir : 📊

➡️ L'expérience demeure le facteur clé de progression salariale dans le domaine SAP.

➡️ La demande pour les consultants SAP qualifiés reste forte et soutenue en France.

➡️ Les salaires varient significativement en fonction de l'expertise et de la spécialisation, notamment sur S/4HANA et les modules recherchés.

🇫🇷 Facteurs Régionaux Influant sur les Salaires en France : 🇫🇷

  • 📍 Région Parisienne & grandes métropoles : Salaires généralement plus élevés en raison du coût de la vie et de la concentration des entreprises.
  • 💼 Type d'entreprise : Les ESN, Cabinets de Conseil, et Clients finaux peuvent proposer des grilles salariales variables.
  • 🗣️ Compétences linguistiques : La maîtrise de l'anglais et d'autres langues étrangères constitue un atout valorisé.

entry.title: Level Up Your SAP Retail Finance Skills! 🚀 Join our Immersive Bootcamp
entry.content:
Are you ready to supercharge your SAP Finance expertise and become a sought-after professional in the dynamic Retail industry? The retail sector is undergoing massive transformation, and skilled SAP Finance experts are crucial for navigating this change and driving success.

That's why we're thrilled to announce our SAP Retail Bootcamp: Finance Overview - your fast track to mastering key financial processes in SAP S/4HANA specifically tailored for retail businesses!

Join us for two intensive days in the beautiful city of Rouen, Normandy, France, packed with practical knowledge, hands-on insights, and expert guidance. This bootcamp is meticulously designed to elevate your SAP Finance skillset and propel your career forward in the exciting world of retail.

Day 1: Building Your Rock-Solid Foundation in SAP Retail Finance

On Day 1, we'll lay the groundwork, focusing on the fundamental building blocks of SAP Retail Finance. You'll gain a deep understanding of the essential organizational structures and core financial accounting processes within a retail context:

  • 🏢 Financial Organizational Units in SAP: Retail Edition: Go beyond the basics and truly understand the nuances of financial organizational units within SAP, specifically as they apply to retail scenarios. We'll explore Company Codes, Controlling Areas, and other critical elements, illustrating how they are structured and utilized in retail businesses with examples and practical exercises. You'll learn how to configure these units to accurately reflect the complex structures of modern retail organizations, from brick-and-mortar stores to e-commerce platforms.

  • 📒 General Ledger (FI-GL): The Retail Finance Heartbeat: Dive into the core of financial accounting in SAP - the General Ledger (FI-GL). We'll explore its retail-specific configurations, chart of accounts adaptations for retail, and how to manage the high volume of transactions characteristic of the retail sector. You'll learn best practices for setting up and utilizing FI-GL to ensure accurate and compliant financial record-keeping in a retail environment.

  • 📈 Profit Centers and Hierarchy: Mastering Retail Profitability Analysis: Profitability is king in retail! This session will equip you with the knowledge to effectively manage and analyze profitability across diverse retail segments and store locations using Profit Centers and Hierarchies. Learn how to configure profit center accounting to track performance by store, product category, region, or any dimension relevant to your retail business. You'll gain practical skills in interpreting profit center data to drive strategic decision-making.

  • 🧾 Accounts Payable (FI-AP): Streamlining Retail Procurement: Efficient vendor invoice processing and payment management are absolutely critical for retail procurement. We'll delve into Accounts Payable (FI-AP), focusing on retail-specific scenarios like handling high volumes of vendor invoices, managing complex payment terms, and optimizing procurement processes. You'll master the techniques to streamline your retail AP processes within SAP, ensuring timely payments and strong vendor relationships.

Day 2: Deep Dive into Retail Finance Operations and Reporting for Strategic Insights

Day 2 takes your expertise to the next level, focusing on core retail finance operations and leveraging SAP S/4HANA for insightful reporting:

  • 💰 Accounts Receivable (FI-AR): Optimizing Retail Sales and Cash Flow: In the fast-paced retail world, efficient customer invoicing and payment processes are essential for healthy cash flow. We'll explore Accounts Receivable (FI-AR) in a retail context, covering topics like managing diverse payment methods, handling high sales volumes, and optimizing processes for both B2C and B2B retail scenarios. You'll learn how to leverage FI-AR to improve your retail sales operations and accelerate cash collection.

  • 📊 Material Costing (CO-PC): Unlocking Retail Article Profitability: Understanding the true cost of retail articles is paramount for pricing strategies and profitability management. This session will uncover the intricacies of Material Costing (CO-PC) in a retail context. We'll explore valuation methods specific to retail inventory, learn how to manage costing for diverse retail articles, and understand the integration between costing and inventory management. You'll gain the skills to analyze retail article costs and contribute to informed pricing and profitability decisions.

  • 🗓️ Period-End Closing (FI/CO): Ensuring Financial Accuracy in Retail: Mastering Period-End Closing processes in FI/CO is crucial for accurate financial reporting. This session will guide you through the essential steps for month-end and year-end closing specifically within SAP Retail Finance. We'll cover retail-specific closing tasks, reconciliation best practices, and how to ensure timely and accurate financial statements for your retail business.

  • 📈 Financial Reporting: Data-Driven Decisions in Retail: The power of SAP S/4HANA lies in its ability to generate insightful financial reports. This final session will focus on how to leverage SAP S/4HANA to create reports tailored for retail analysis and strategic decision-making. You'll discover how to extract key retail KPIs, build custom reports, and utilize SAP's reporting tools to gain actionable insights from your financial data, empowering data-driven decisions in your retail organization.

Why Attend This Immersive SAP Retail Finance Bootcamp?

  • Hands-on, Practical Learning: This isn't just theory! Our bootcamp is designed for practical application. You'll gain real-world skills and techniques that you can immediately implement in your SAP Retail Finance role, making you more effective and valuable from day one.
  • Expert-Led Sessions: Learn directly from experienced SAP consultants who possess deep knowledge of both SAP Finance and the Retail industry. Benefit from their real-world project experience, best practices, and practical tips and tricks.
  • Boost Your Career Trajectory: In today's competitive job market, specialized skills are in high demand. By enhancing your SAP Finance skillset with a retail focus, you'll become a highly sought-after retail finance expert, opening doors to exciting career opportunities and advancement.
  • Valuable Networking Opportunities: Connect and collaborate with fellow SAP professionals who are also passionate about the retail space. Expand your professional network, share experiences, and build valuable connections within the SAP retail community in Rouen, Normandy, France.

Don't miss this exceptional opportunity to accelerate your SAP Retail Finance journey! Spaces are limited for this immersive bootcamp in Rouen, Normandy, France.

Register today and take the next step towards becoming a leading SAP Retail Finance expert!

entry.title: Sales order to sales order copy controls VTAA
entry.content:
As per the business requirement while the creating a sales order with reference to another sales order the request delivery date and pricing date should not be copy from the reference document, it should be the present date.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Sales order to sales order copy controls VTAA
entry.content:
Hello,
 
The requested delivery date VBAK-VDATU is taken over in General header data copy control routine 051 (FV45C051).
The pricing date (VBKD-PRSDT) is taken over in Item Business Data copy control routine 102 (FV45C102).
 
To change the standard behavior you need to create a copy of the above mentioned routines and modify the logic to avoid copying the fields that you do not want to take from the reference document. Then assign the custom routines in transaction VTAA.

Best regards,
Edina
SAP

entry.title: Re: Issue with Loading/Creating Story Reports in Non-Prod...
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have encountered the same earlier, now its been resolved; Look for the below workarounds.

1. Kindly Enable 3rd party cookies in any browser first, clear the cache and history - Should work most of the times. 

2. Check with your IT Team or Integrations Team - Ask them to check the provisioning logs, it should be all passed, even there is one user which has FAILED, your stories wont work. 

 

Regards
Ajay Kulkarni

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 4: Why governance and administration in BTP?
entry.content:
This episode discusses the importance of governance and administration in SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform). As more services and applications move to the cloud, like Work Zone and Integration Suite, it's crucial to have a structured and clean BTP landscape. The challenges of limited experience, knowledge gaps and poor structuring of sub-accounts are highlighted. The episode emphasizes best practices in subdomain naming, directory organization and shared responsibilities between developers and administrators. It also touches on the impact of cost, security and ownership when managing a BTP environment effectively.

Takeaways 

  • BTP adoption is growing rapidly with more services and applications moving to the platform.
  • A good, clean and structured BTP landscape is crucial as more services migrate to the platform.
  • Many lack experience and knowledge about starting, structuring, and maintaining BTP environments effectively.
  • Although documentation exists, it often lacks best practices and recommendations for governance and administration in BTP.
  • Poor structuring of subaccounts is a common issue
  • Governance in BTP requires clear ownership and responsibility, as different roles (e.g., developers, administrators) share duties.
  • Security and cost management are often overlooked by both developers and administrators, leading to potential issues.
  • The subdomain generation process in BTP can create subdomains that are not user-friendly, so it's better to create meaningful subdomains.
  • Proper naming conventions for subdomains are essential to indicate the environment (e.g., development, production).
  • Directories and subaccounts should be clearly organized to avoid confusion, particularly when working with multiple environments.
  • Governance and administration in BTP require constant attention to ensure systems remain efficient, secure, and cost-effective.

entry.title: SAP IPS, alter Groupnames in Transformation and assign to them
entry.content:
Hello,

We are facing a situation where our AD groups for our cloud target systems in SAP IBP have "DEV," "TST," or "PRD" in their names. These names need to be removed when reading from the source, and then the role should be assigned to the user. We are doing this to use the transport function for the roles, as they will then have the same name in all target systems. I have adjusted the transformation as follows. It runs without any errors, but the roles are not being assigned to the users:

    "group": {
        "mappings": [
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
                "targetVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem",
                "scope": "createEntity",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "replaceString",
                        "target": "TST-",
                        "replacement": ""
                    },
                    {
                        "function": "replaceString",
                        "target": "DEV-",
                        "replacement": ""
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
                "targetPath": "$.displayName",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "replaceString",
                        "target": "TST-",
                        "replacement": ""
                    },
                    {
                        "function": "replaceString",
                        "target": "DEV-",
                        "replacement": ""
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.members[*].value",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.members[?(@.value)]",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "resolveEntityIds"
                    }
                ]
            }
        ]
    }

entry.title: How to determine if a BAPI execution is successful or not?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm new to BAPIs and trying to understand the best way to determine whether a BAPI execution was successful. I have a few questions regarding this:

  1. Is the RETURN array the correct field to always check for execution status? If so, are there any standard rules for interpreting it? For example:

    • If any entry in the RETURN array has TYPE = 'E', consider it a failure.
    • If all entries have TYPE = 'S', consider it a success.
    • How should we interpret other types like W (Warning) or I (Information)?
    • What should be considered when the RETURN array is completely empty? Does an empty RETURN array always indicate success?

  2. Is there a better or more reliable way to determine the execution status of a BAPI?

Any insights or best practices would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: You are not authorized to buy. Contact your administr...
entry.content:
I found the note 3180259 which explains the behaviour :

Cause

A user was acting as the Purchasing User to create a requisition.  This is not allowed in Guided Buying production and test environments.  

Resolution

Additional Information

A fix was deployed, GB-4973, which changed the behavior when acting as a user in Guided Buying. Users will now see the "You are not authorized to buy. Contact your administrator." message while they are acting as another user in Guided Buying to create a requisition. This behavior was implemented so the Guided Buying application would behave the same way as the Ariba Buying production application where a user is not allowed to create a transactional document while acting as another user. 

Please also note, the behavior change in Guided Buying only occurs when users login directly and then Act as another user to go to Guided Buying.  The On Behalf Of functionality is not affected. Users can login to Guided Buying and then request to buy on behalf of other user.  We would suggest to use the Request on behalf of functionality to create requests for other users in Guided Buying.

entry.title: Re: Consuming CDS View Entities Using ODBC-Based Client Tools
entry.content:
You do not have to create a SICF entry. However on On Prem systems you have to make sure that all profile parameters are set correctly. This is mentioned in the On Prem documentation but you may have missed it. 

The required parameters are:
wssql/active = 1
wssql/privileged/active = 1

You can also use transaction DBC2ABAP_UTILS to check your local SQL service configuration. This transaction also includes a SQL console that allows you to test SQL service SQL statements.
Regards
                Frank

entry.title: Re: How to determine if a BAPI execution is successful or...
entry.content:
Basically

  • If RETURN contains an error message of type 'E' or an abort message of type 'A', then the BAPI execution has failed, aborted (time for BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK).
  • Otherwise, the BAPI was able to process something, but some 'W' or even 'S' and 'I' messages may contain information such as 'field xxx was not changed', but this depends on the BAPI. (time for BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT)

Your application could store the contents of RETURN in an application log to be viewed and analysed later with SLG1.

entry.title: Re: SAP - DMC wrong inspection point valuation for process order
entry.content: Dear Customer! Is the inspection point valuation correctly set to "Rejected" in S/4HANA and wrongly displayed in SAP DM ? If so, please open a ticket and share the integration message and sample details for reproducing the issue. However, if the inspection point valuation is the same in SAP DM and S/4HANA then please check your QM configuration and master data settings in S/4HANA (sampling procedure with valuation mode, inspection point definition in S/4HANA (IMG) and in SAP DM, and furthermore please check whether the setting "automatic valuation" is set for inspection points in the routing operation details). All of these settings could influence that an inspection point is accepted even if all characteristics are rejected. Thank you. Best regards, Manfred

entry.title: Re: Product Compliance (new): automatic substance checks not starting with mass uploaded materials
entry.content:
Hello @Paulo_Pinto,

I am afraid that we will need to isolate and analyse the issue step by step.

  1. Create a "Test Migration" project to migrate data. The main entry point to check availability and documentation for existing migration objects is 
    1. Public Cloud: https://help.sap.com/S4_CE_MO
    2. Private Cloud / OP: https://help.sap.com/S4_OP_MO
  2. You need to migrate the data bottom up, starting from substance, raw material, up to the product and compositions. The migration object for unpackaged products is described here - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/d3a3eb7caa1842858bf0372e17ad3909/a35fb7ecebfa4c12b1ec02f2bec2e662.html?version=2023.002 . Please make sure that you consider the sequence / prerequisite Migration objects as well as assigning the proper Compliance Purposes to the product and raw materials which contain the Compliance Requirements for which you want to executed a compliance assessment.
  3. Import the migration project and after the import open the app F4005 - Process Product Compliance Events and search for the proper events in the monitor to figure out if an event has been raised. From here you can check if the follow-on processing is executed or not.
    1. If the events are not processed, you need to check the job log and application log in SLG1 for log object EHFND_PCEP (events) respectively EHFND_PCTP (tasks) together with the tools mentioned by @sergey_nozhenko 
    2. If you see the events and have the impression that they are not being executed or the execution is not proceeding please check for SAP notes for OP2023 and your support package. There are some notes available and since i do not know your SP they could be applicable - e.g. search for PCEP and restrict by your Software components / SP.
    3. If you do not see any events at all then this may be an indicator that the root cause for your symptom is somewhere in the migration object.
    4. BTW - you can use the same app F4005 to compare the output / events and tasks if you manually perform the change within an APP. However the completed events/logs etc. are deleted after 2 days.

If you still cannot find the Root cause then please create a case on behalf of your customer, so that the experts can do the analysis directly in the system.

Last but not lease i have read that you came from OP2021 and that you faced some hurdles in the documentation. We are striving hard to document everything as thorough as possible. But if you have feedback, that important information is missing or difficult to understand you can add your findings in the case as well or get directly in contact with me - kristian.mitic@sap.com.

Best regards,

Kristian

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud reference is present in S/4HA...
entry.content: so the basic difference is that: the replication target is on object store and the method is a Push method, and the job pushes the data from S/4 to HDLFS instead of memory. The main mechanism that extract delta keys (with triggers), propagate them from master logging tables to subscriber logging tables (observer job), reconstruct CDS Views output in RMS/RDB, is the same. Potentialy it will be slower as there is a .CSV file generation and maybe worse compression during data transfer... still the notes are not yet released.

entry.title: Connecting Shopify to CAPM Issue
entry.content:
Hello All,


I’m currently working on a CAPM (Cloud Application Programming Model) project, where I’m trying to integrate Shopify using its API endpoints. The goal is to retrieve data by passing query parameters while using authentication credentials such as:

  • X-Shopify-Access-Token
  • Content-Type: application/json

Everything seems straightforward, but when I attempt to make a request, I keep running into this error:
read tcp 100.96.8.158:41620->23.227.38.74:443: read: connection reset by peer\

  • Checked API Credentials – The X-Shopify-Access-Token is valid, and the headers are correctly set.
  • Verified API Endpoint – The Shopify API URL and query parameters seem correct.
  • Tested with Postman – Surprisingly, the request works fine in Postman, but fails within CAPM.
  • Network & Firewall Debugging –
    • No restrictions from my end.
    • The Shopify endpoint is reachable via browser and Postman.
    • No VPN or proxy causing issues.
  • Tried Different HTTP Libraries –
    • Used both axios and node-fetch, but got the same error.
    • The issue persists in both @SAP-cloud-sdk/http-client and direct http.request().
  • Looked at Shopify Rate Limits –
    • Shopify enforces rate limits, but I’m well under the threshold.
    • Even after waiting, the request fails again.

      Here I'm sharing the screenshots of it and code which I'm using Can anyone help on this?

Sample Code:



Issue:

Thanks,
Manoj Kumar.

entry.title: Re: How to determine if a BAPI execution is successful or not?
entry.content: General way (but not always): at least one E message means an error. A success otherwise. There might be some exceptions, in that case search in the ABAP documentation, in the SAP Help portal, in the SAP notes or in the forum.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere: Table Function - Union with Tuple instead...
entry.content:
Hello,

I have not tested this logic in Datasphere, but in other systems (e.g. BW/4) you select from dummy table. See example in the following blog:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/use-of-dummy-table-for-mocking-and-testing-logic/ba-p/13545565
Hope this helps.

entry.title: Issue with outbound IDoc PEXR2003/PEXR2001 Missing segment and Unable to debug.
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently facing an issue wherein the outbound IDOC of Base Type PEXR2003, We have a segment E1IDUU2 needs to be filled. But The parent segment E1IDPU3 and E1IDPU1 is not getting filled either. When we tried to debug the IDocs in the usual way when the payment is processed from F110 t-code, it doesn't trigger any of the ABAP breakpoints and after a lot of research, I understand that FI Idocs can't be triggered in the same way where SD and MM IDocs are triggered. Since all the blogs that I searched were almost two decades ago, I wanted to see if anyone has solution for my following queries.

I have couple questions.

1. How to trigger the FI IDoc to debug?

2. The idoc is standard without any customization in User Exits (Checked already), from which table does the E1IDPU3 additional sub item data is mapped from or do we need enhancement to fill these fields?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Message Output Types configuration
entry.content:
Message Output Types configuration.

Objective is to configure the message output determination and test the output in the Purchase Order.

Step 1: Create Condition Table

SPRO Path: SAP IMG -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Messages -> Output Control -> Condition Tables -> Define Condition Table for Purchase Order.

Define Condition Table for Purchase Order.

We created a new condition Table 903 with Purchase Org/Document Type/Purchase Group

Activate the condition Table by pressing the icon 

Condition Table created:

Step 2: Create Access Sequence

SPRO Path: SAP IMG -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Messages -> Output Control -> Access Sequence -> Define Access Sequence for Purchase Order

Create a new access sequence as per the business requirement.

Enter the Table which the access sequence would refer and mark the Exclusive indicator.

Step 3: Create Message Types

SPRO Path: SAP IMG -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Messages -> Output Control -> Message Types -> Define Message Types for Purchase Order.

a. Maintain Message Types:

Create Message Types and assign the Access sequence.

b. Fine-Tuned Control: Purchase Order

Step 4: Message Determination Schemas

SPRO Path: SAP IMG -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Messages -> Output Control -> Message Determination Schemas -> Define Message Schema for Purchase Order.

a. Maintain Message Determination Schema: Purchase Order

Assign the Output Type in the Procedure.

b. Assign Schema to Purchase Order

Step 5: Partner Roles per Message Type

SPRO Path: SAP IMG -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Messages -> Output Control -> Partner Roles per Message Type -> Define Partner Roles for Purchase Order

Step 6: Partner Function Setup

a. Define Permissible Partner Roles per Account Group

SPRO Path: SAP IMG -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Partner Determination -> Partner Roles -> Define Permissible Partner Roles per Account Group

b. Partner Settings in Supplier Master Record

SPRO Path: SAP IMG -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Partner Determination -> Partner Roles -> Assign Partner Schemas to Account Groups

Assign the partner schemas to the vendor account groups.

c. Partner Settings in Purchasing Documents

SPRO Path: SAP IMG -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Partner Determination -> Partner Settings in Purchasing Documents -> Assign Partner Schemas to Document Types.

Partner Schema assigned to Purchasing document category.

Step 7: BP With partner Functions

Step 8: Creation of Output Condition Records.

Step 9: Transaction

a. PO Creation:

b. Output triggered and appearing in Message Output:

With Regards,

Sagnik.

entry.title: Inventory Management: Inventory Counting Process - Cycle Counting (IM-MM)
entry.content:
What is inventory counting?

Basically, this is a process of verifying and recording the actual stock levels in a storage location or warehouse to ensure they match the data in the system. In physical counting, we need to shut down the entire operation and count everything. Until everything’s counted, we cannot resume executing any operation like picking, packing, and shipping.

Counting Physical Inventory Flow

 

Now let’s talk about cycle counting. 

Cycle counting is a method of counting inventory periodically based on predefined criteria, such as the frequency set by ABC classification. This counting is where a subset of inventory items is counted on a rotating schedule rather than conducting a full physical inventory at once. It helps in maintaining inventory accuracy while minimizing operational disruptions.

ABC cycle counting uses the Pareto Principal method to prioritize inventory into several tiers by the level of importance. Items that generate the most revenue are counted most frequently.

For example, a fast moving and high value material will be counted several times a year while slow moving and low value material can be counted once a year. Here is the example calculation:

Example calculation of ABC cycle counting

SAP will calculate the value for each material in the plant based on the consumption value, requirement value, and movement type. Here are the steps to see the ABC Cycle Counting analysis:

ABC cycle counting in SAP

 

  • Based on movement type:
    1. Configuration: (T-Code: OMCO)

    Configuration

    Number of cycle counts: Number of times / year that you want to count the material.
    PCT (%) Percentage: % of materials that you assign to ABC classification, ranking from high to low.

    2. Set Indicator: MM01 (Plant data/Stor.1 Tab)

    You can set the cycle counting indicator manually from the material master.

    Cycle counting in material master

    Physical inventory indicator for cycle counting indicates that the given material is subject to the cycle counting method of inventory. The indicator also defines at which time intervals a physical inventory is to be carried out for the material.


    CC indicator is fixed, specifies that the cycle counting indicator cannot be changed for cycle counting by an ABC analysis. The only way to change this indicator is through manual maintenance of the material master record. Set this indicator if the material is always to remain in the specified cycle counting category.

    3. ABC Analysis: (T-Code: MIBC)

    Analysis based on consumption value

    Consumptions in MIBC are based on movement types and on configuration settings for movement types.


    Summary of counting effort per fiscal year

    If you click the Display Counting Effort, you will see the summary of how many materials in every category and how many times they need to be counted in one plant. This will help businesses to decide the inventory counting schedule without taking time to do manual analysis.

  • Based on Consumption
    1.     Configuration: SPRO → Logistic → Material Master → Settings for Key Fields → Extend ABC Indicator

    ABC indicator for materials

    We can set the indicator based on what the business needs. This indicator classifies a material as an A, B, or C part according to its consumption value.

    2. Set Indicator: (T-Code: MM01 (MRP 1))

    ABC indicator in material master MRP 1

    3. ABC Analysis: (T-Code: MC40)

    Analysis strategy

    This report provides ABC Analysis based on the consumption / usage of material.

    Analysis based on the consumption / usage value

    You can also see the double-line analysis that shows the quantity. This will help businesses to decide which material needs to be counted more frequently based on the consumption.

  • Based on requirement value
    The configuration and where to set the indicator are the same. But for the analysis result, you can check it on MC41.

    ABC Analysis: (T-Code: MC41)

    Analysis strategy

    This report provides ABC Analysis based on the requirement (MRP) of material.

    Analysis based on the requirement (MRP) of material

    Then, how to enter and adjust inventory counting?

    Firstly, we need to know there are several stock types in an inventory. Each type will have a different movement type based on the stock's conditions. If there is any difference with the book stocks after counting the physical stocks, the book stocks need to be adjusted to the physical stocks. 

    Movement types

    Now we’re going to talk about how to do the inventory counting in SAP.

    Here are the steps to enter the inventory counting:

    Inventory counting process flow


    1. Go to MI01 to create an inventory document.

    Create PI document

    Posting Block = Indicates that no goods movements involving the materials listed in the physical inventory document can be posted for the duration of a physical inventory count.

    Stock Type = Shows the stock type for which a physical inventory is to be carried out (Warehouse, Quality Inspection, Warehouse/Quality Inspection, Blocked).

    2. Perform counting and enter the inventory counting result in MI04.
    Insert the physical inventory document number that has been created before.

    Enter inventory count

    Insert the physical inventory quantity that has been counted. Specifies the quantity to be moved in the unit of entry. The quantity is automatically converted to the stockkeeping unit (base unit of measure).

    Enter quantity

    3. Checklist of Inventory Differences in MI20.
    The difference quantity between the physical inventory and system stock will be shown in this window.

    List of difference

    The aim of posting differences is to reconcile stocks in the ERP system and in particular, in financial accounting.
    List of inventory differences

    4. Post inventory differences in MI07

    Post inventory differences: Initial screen

    Reason for inventory difference = Choose the reason based on the movement type. The movement type can be defined by the stock's condition.

    Post inventory differences

    5. Check material document in MMBE (Not mandatory)

    In the stock overview, you can select the stocks that have been entered based on the physical inventory quantity and see the material document. There you will see that the materials are reduced by 5 pcs. Using the right movement type will automatically execute the adjustment stocks between book stocks and physical stocks.

    Material document list

    6. Post Document Count and Differences in MI10

    Post document

    7. Print preview in MI21 (Not mandatory)

    Print preview


    So that's basically how you do the cycle counting in SAP. By implementing cycle counting, organizations can systematically verify inventory levels without requiring a full physical count, reducing operational disruptions while improving data accuracy. SAP cycle counting process supports continuous inventory accuracy, leading to better decision-making and optimized operational performance.

    Feel free to discuss more if you have any other insights or questions!



    Cheers,
    Dafina

entry.title: Purchase Info Record
entry.content:
I. INTRODUCTION
A Purchase Info Record (PIR) in SAP is a crucial tool that bridges the gap between purchasing and procurement efficiency. It serves as a database of key details about a vendor and the materials they supply, including pricing, lead times, and terms. By maintaining accurate PIRs, companies can streamline procurement, optimize costs, and enhance supplier relationships.

(source: Technosap)

--> Guide: Maintain Info Record

1. Create Info Record (ME11)

2. Fill the purchase organization data

3. Fill the mandatory

4. Next step, you can choose "Conditions"

5. Check the standard amount and unit, you can choose "Scales" for adding a packet

6. Example for the scale quantity: if we buy 100 quantity the amount will be cheaper

--> Guide: Implementation on PO

1. Create PO "ME21N", you can see the net price will automatically generated base on PIR

2. Change the quantity to 100 to activate the scales 

--> Guide: GR-Based-IV-Function

1. Check your Info Record (ME12)

2. If not activated

3. In purchase order (ME21N) will automatically not activated too (follow the info record)

4. If activated

5. In purchase order (ME21N) will automatically activated too

So when to activate it and when not to?:

-> Use GR-Based Invoice Verification when:

  1. You want invoices to match the goods receipt quantity rather than the purchase order quantity.
  2. Goods are received in multiple deliveries, and the invoice should correspond to each partial delivery.
  3. Quality checks are necessary before payment—ensuring only received and accepted goods are invoiced.
  4. For materials that require inspection before being invoiced (e.g., raw materials, bulk shipments).

To prevent overpayment, ensuring invoices match received quantities instead of just ordered amounts.

-> Avoid GR-Based Invoice Verification when:

  1. Invoices should be posted based on the purchase order (PO) without waiting for goods receipt (e.g., services).
  2. Freight or additional costs are involved that don't depend on the received quantity.
  3. You are using Evaluated Receipt Settlement (ERS)—since ERS invoices are generated automatically based on GR.
  4. For non-stock materials where invoices are received first, and goods movement is secondary.

For blanket purchase orders (BPOs) where the invoice is based on agreed terms rather than GR.

--> Guide: Effect GR-BSD-IV on Invoice

1. Make PO with GR not activated

2. Confirm Invoice, the item automatically generated without post a GR first

3. Make purchase order with GR activated

4. Post GR

5. Create invoice after post a GR

--> Conclusion

A Purchase Info Record (PIR) is a key element in SAP procurement, acting as a bridge between vendors and purchasing data. It ensures accurate pricing, lead times, and order conditions, streamlining procurement operations.

Activating GR-Based Invoice Verification (GR-BSD-IV) in PIR helps enforce strict invoice control based on received goods, reducing discrepancies and preventing overpayment. However, it should be used selectively, depending on the nature of procurement—whether it involves stock materials, services, or blanket POs.

By effectively managing PIRs, businesses can optimize supplier relationships, enhance cost control, and improve overall procurement efficiency. 🚀

Thank you very much, i hope this knowledge is helpfull

entry.title: How to Release PR document from HOLD or PARK status using BAPI
entry.content:
Introduction:

A purchase requisition is an internal document used in an organization to request the procurement of goods or services. In SAP, efficient management of purchase requisitions is crucial for smooth procurement operations. This blog will provide a clear guide on releasing PR documents from HOLD or PARK status using BAPIs and releasing the document for further processing.

 

Understanding Parked and Hold Documents in SAP:

First, let's understand the PARK and HOLD options in SAP. These functionalities are essential for managing purchasing documents, allowing users to save them temporarily for later processing.

Hold: If the document has incorrect or incomplete data and you want to save it temporarily to process later, use the Hold option.

Park: If the document has complete and correct data but needs further workflow approvals or budget checks, use the Park functionality.

 

Activating Hold and Park options:

To activate the PARK and HOLD options in transactions ME51N, ME52N, and ME53N, follow these two steps:

  1. Activate Business Functionality
    • Go to T-Code SPRO.
    • Click on Activate Business Functions

       

    • Expand the folder Enterprise_Business_Functions and activate the functionality LOG_MM_CI_3.

       

     2. Enable PARK and HOLD Features In T-Code SPRO, follow this path:
          Materials Management --> Purchasing --> Environment Data --> Activate 'Park and Hold' in Purchasing Documents. 

 By completing these steps, you will enable the PARK and HOLD functionalities for purchasing documents in your SAP system.

 

Changing PR document status from HOLD or PARKED to SAVED using BAPI:

To change the status of a purchase requisition document from HOLD or PARKED to SAVED, you can use the BAPI “BAPI_PR_CHANGE.”. 

Below is an example of how to implement this in ABAP:

 

 

DATA: ls_prheader  TYPE bapimereqheader,
      ls_prheaderx TYPE bapimereqheaderx,
      lt_return    TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2.

IF  ls_prheader-preq_no IS NOT INITIAL.

 ls_prheader = VALUE #( preq_no       = |{ ls_prheader-preq_no ALPHA = IN }|
                        park_complete = abap_false
                        hold_complete = abap_false
                        memorytype    = abap_false ).
  
 ls_prheaderx = VALUE #( preq_no       = abap_true
                         park_complete = abap_true
                         hold_complete = abap_true
                         memorytype    = abap_true ).

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PR_CHANGE'
  EXPORTING
   number     = ls_prheader-preq_no “Your PR Number
    prheader  = ls_prheader
    prheaderx = ls_prheaderx
  TABLES
    return   = lt_return.
IF VALUE #( lt_return[ type = 'S' ] OPTIONAL ) IS NOT INITIAL.
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
       wait = abap_true.
ELSE.
    ROLLBACK WORK.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

 

 

 

 

Release Strategy:

The release strategy in SAP defines the approval process for purchase requisitions:

  • Purpose: Ensures PRs are reviewed and approved before conversion to purchase orders.
  • Release Codes: Each strategy can have up to eight release codes, indicating who must approve the requisition.
  • Approval Sequence: Specifies the order in which approvals must be obtained.
  • Assignment: Release strategies are assigned based on release conditions like document type and value.

 

Release Code:

A release code is a two-character ID that authorizes a person to approve or release a purchase requisition or external purchasing document.

  • Purpose: It serves as an electronic signature, replacing traditional handwritten approvals. This digitization streamlines the approval process and enhances efficiency.
  • Assignment: Release codes are assigned to employees based on their roles and authorization levels within the organization. For example, a clerk might have a release code for initial approvals, while a manager has a higher-level code.
  • Configuration:
      • You can define up to eight release codes within a release strategy.
      • Each code is linked to specific approval levels, ensuring that the correct personnel review PRs based on their value or other criteria.
  • Approval Process: The release strategy specifies which release codes must be used and the sequence of approvals required.

 

Releasing PR documents using BAPI:

To release a PR that needs approval from respective employees/approvers in your organization, you can use the BAPI - “BAPI_REQUISITION_RELEASE_GEN”.

Below is an example implementation:

 

 

 

DATA : lt_return TYPE TABLE OF bapireturn.
 
 CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_REQUISITION_RELEASE_GEN'
   EXPORTING
     number     = iv_pr_num       “Your PR Number
     rel_code   = iv_release_code “Release code
   TABLES
     return      = lt_return.
 IF VALUE #( lt_return[ type = 'S' ] OPTIONAL ) IS NOT INITIAL OR 
    lt_return IS INITIAL.
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
       wait = abap_true.
 ELSE.
    ROLLBACK WORK.
 ENDIF.

 

 

 

 

Finding Release Codes:

You can find release codes under the Release Strategy tab in transactions ME51N, ME52N, and ME53N.

The following tables are useful for retrieving relevant information:

  • EBAN: To get the release group and release strategy for the PR number.
  • T16FS: To get release codes for a particular release group & strategy.
  • T16FW: To get approver names/IDs for a release code.

 

Conclusion:

This blog emphasized the significance of parked and hold documents, the activation of essential functionalities, and the use of BAPIs to change PR statuses. We also explored the release procedure and strategy, illustrating how structured approvals can enhance efficiency in your procurement processes.

Thank you for reading!
